tool_args.label: NYCT Key Performance Metrics June 2025
tool_args.raw_text: New York City Transit Key Performance Metrics June 2025

payload.title: NEW YORK CITY TRANSIT KEY PERFORMANCE METRICS
payload.prepared_for: June 2025 meeting of the New York City Transit & Bus Committee

tool_args.label: Demetrius Crichlow, President of New York City Transit
tool_args.raw_text: Demetrius Crichlow President New York City Transit

payload.role: President of New York City Transit

tool_args.label: Janno Lieber, MTA Chair & CEO
tool_args.raw_text: MTA Chair & CEO Janno Lieber

tool_args.label: Donovan Richards, Queens Borough President
tool_args.raw_text: Queens Borough President Donovan Richards

tool_args.label: Metropolitan Transportation Authority (MTA)
tool_args.raw_text: The Metropolitan Transportation Authority is North America's largest transportation network, serving a population of 15.3 million people across a 5,000-square-mile travel area surrounding New York City through Long Island, southeastern New York State, and Connecticut.

payload.service_area_description: serving a population of 15.3 million people across a 5,000-square-mile travel area surrounding New York City through Long Island, southeastern New York State, and Connecticut

tool_args.label: New York City Transit (NYCT) and MTA Bus
tool_args.raw_text: New York City Transit and MTA Bus operate all subways and buses in New York City. Our 45,000 employees serve 4,500,000 passengers a day. We operate nearly 6,700 subway cars and 5,800 buses, and we maintain 472 subway stations, 640 miles of track, 27 bus depots and 70 shops and yards.

payload.entity_name: New York City Transit (NYCT) and MTA Bus

tool_args.label: Queens Bus Network Redesign
tool_args.raw_text: On June 29, we will officially begin Phase 1 of the Queens Bus Network Redesign. This is a momentous occasion. It marks the culmination of six years of research, planning, and outreach.

payload.description: Reimagined bus network for Queens, driven by customer feedback, implementing Rush routes and modernized service across the borough

tool_args.label: Jamaica Bus Depot Rebuild and Expansion
tool_args.raw_text: The MTA is rebuilding and expanding the Jamaica Bus Depot in Queens to better serve the community and support a modern, growing bus fleet. Once complete, the upgraded depot will handle the operation, maintenance, and storage of up to 300 buses.

payload.project_name: Jamaica Bus Depot Rebuild and Expansion
payload.sustainability_features: green roof, LEED certification standards, stormwater detention tanks (3,600 feet of storm drain and fuel piping, 30,000 cubic feet of stormwater detention tanks)
payload.noise_mitigation: sound-reducing walls along 107th Avenue and 165th Street
payload.local_hiring_goal: 20% of NY State workforce from Southeast Queens

tool_args.label: Jamaica Bus Terminal Relocation
tool_args.raw_text: The terminal is moving from Merrick Boulevard to a new, state-of-the-art facility on 90th Avenue.

payload.description: Relocation of Jamaica Bus Terminal from Merrick Boulevard to a new state-of-the-art facility on 90th Avenue

tool_args.label: F / M Subway Service Swap (F/M Swap)
tool_args.raw_text: Later this year, we'll be updating the subway map again, for the second time in 2025, with a new service adjustment. We're calling it the F / M swap.

payload.description: F train joins E along 53rd Street tunnel serving Jackson Heights, Queens Plaza, and Court Square; M takes over 63rd Street segment serving Roosevelt Island and 21st Street–Queensbridge
payload.benefits: increased reliability, reduced delays, shorter travel times; 2.5 times more riders benefit than those with longer trips

tool_args.label: Subway Weekday On-Time Performance May 2025
tool_args.raw_text: In May 2025, weekday On-Time Performance (OTP) was 85.2%.

payload.context: Highest subway OTP since automated tracking began

tool_args.label: Subway OTP improvement from April 2025
tool_args.raw_text: This represents a 0.7 percentage point improvement over April of 2025

payload.raw_value_text: 0.7 percentage point improvement over April of 2025

tool_args.label: Subway Weekend On-Time Performance May 2025
tool_args.raw_text: Weekend OTP was 86.7%.

tool_args.label: Customer Journey Time Performance May 2025
tool_args.raw_text: The percentage of customers arriving at their destinations within five minutes of schedule (CJTP) improved to 86.1%

payload.context: Best CJTP performance since metric was introduced, outside of COVID

tool_args.label: Elevator Availability May 2025
tool_args.raw_text: Elevator availability remained near its record high at 98.4%

tool_args.label: Escalator Availability May 2025
tool_args.raw_text: escalator availability was 95.8%

tool_args.label: Additional Platform Time May 2025
tool_args.raw_text: May 2025 APT 1.4 ATT 0.5 Total 1.9

tool_args.label: Additional Train Time (Subway) May 2025
tool_args.raw_text: May 2025 ATT 0.5

tool_args.label: Bus Service Delivered May 2025
tool_args.raw_text: Service Delivered remained steady at 95.2%

tool_args.label: Bus Customer Journey Time Performance May 2025
tool_args.raw_text: Customer Journey Time Performance improved by 1.1% to 69.5%

tool_args.label: Average Bus Speed May 2025
tool_args.raw_text: Average bus speeds increased to 8.0 mph, marking a 1.3% improvement over the prior year.

tool_args.label: Paratransit Completed Trips May 2025
tool_args.raw_text: We served nearly 1.35 million riders on 940,000 completed trips last month.

tool_args.label: Paratransit Riders Served May 2025
tool_args.raw_text: We served nearly 1.35 million riders on 940,000 completed trips last month.

tool_args.label: Paratransit Year-over-Year Growth May 2025
tool_args.raw_text: May 2025 marked another set of milestones as Paratransit continues to see record setting trips and ridership, with an increase in both of more than 17% when compared to May 2024.

payload.raw_value_text: increase in both of more than 17% when compared to May 2024

tool_args.label: Paratransit Broker On-Time Performance May 2025
tool_args.raw_text: broker service completed 93% of the trips within the 20-minute pickup window from promise time

tool_args.label: Paratransit Primary Carrier On-Time Performance May 2025
tool_args.raw_text: our primary carrier service completed 85% of trips within the 20-minute pickup window from promise time

payload.metric_name: Paratransit Primary Carrier On-Time Performance (20-min window)

tool_args.raw_text: The May 2025 no-show rate was 1.00 per 1,000 trips for primary service

tool_args.label: Paratransit Broker No-Show Rate May 2025
tool_args.raw_text: 0.80 per 1,000 trips for broker service

tool_args.label: Paratransit Call Center Answer Rate May 2025
tool_args.raw_text: 94% of all calls were answered

tool_args.label: Paratransit Average Answer Speed May 2025
tool_args.raw_text: the average answer speed was 70 seconds

payload.metric_name: Paratransit Call Center Average Answer Speed

tool_args.label: Estimated Paid Subway Ridership May 2025
tool_args.raw_text: Paid weekday subway ridership is estimated to have been 4.192 million, a new post-COVID monthly high

tool_args.label: Subway Paid Ridership Year-over-Year Growth May 2025
tool_args.raw_text: a 7.1% increase over May 2024

payload.metric_name: Subway Paid Weekday Ridership Year-over-Year Change

tool_args.label: Estimated Paid Bus Ridership May 2025
tool_args.raw_text: the paid weekday total is estimated at 1.48 million

tool_args.raw_text: 13.1% higher than the 1.30 million recorded in May of 2024

payload.metric_name: Bus Paid Weekday Ridership Year-over-Year Change
payload.raw_value_text: 13.1% higher than the 1.30 million recorded in May of 2024

tool_args.label: MY AAR App Booking Share May 2025
tool_args.raw_text: We also had more than 21% of trips booked on the MY AAR app

payload.raw_value_text: more than 21% of trips booked on the MY AAR app

tool_args.label: ACE Average Bus Speed Improvement
tool_args.raw_text: Across the city, ACE improves bus speeds by over 5% on average.

payload.metric_name: Automated Camera Enforcement (ACE) Average Bus Speed Improvement

tool_args.label: ACE Bx35 Bus Speed Improvement
tool_args.raw_text: Let's look at the Bx35, where ACE has increased speeds by 20%.

tool_args.label: ACE + 181st Street Busway Corridor Speed Improvement
tool_args.raw_text: Combined with the 181st Street busway, buses in that corridor are now moving 40% faster than they were before.

payload.metric_name: Bx35 Corridor (ACE + 181st Street Busway) Speed Improvement

tool_args.label: ACE Collision Reduction
tool_args.raw_text: On average, routes with ACE have seen 20% fewer collisions

tool_args.label: Automated Camera Enforcement (ACE) Program
tool_args.raw_text: Last month, we expanded ACE to five new routes: the M2, M4, M42, M100, and Bx5.

tool_args.label: Queens Bus Network Redesign Phase 1 Launch
tool_args.raw_text: On June 29, we will officially begin Phase 1 of the Queens Bus Network Redesign.

payload.event_name: Queens Bus Network Redesign Phase 1 Launch

tool_args.label: 168th St/Jamaica Interim Bus Terminal Opens
tool_args.raw_text: this month the MTA officially relocated bus operations to the new interim 168th Street Bus Terminal in Jamaica, Queens, effective Sunday, June 1, 2025.

payload.description: Relocation of bus operations to new interim 168th Street Bus Terminal in Jamaica, Queens

tool_args.label: Paratransit All-Time Single-Day Record
tool_args.raw_text: Also, on June 11, our Paratransit service hit an all-time record, with 46,251 trips scheduled in a single day.

payload.description: Paratransit hit an all-time record with 46,251 trips scheduled in a single day

tool_args.label: Customer Ambassador Program Start
tool_args.raw_text: The MTA has initiated a Customer Ambassador Program that started Monday, June 16, to assist riders ahead of Phase 1 of the Queens Bus Network Redesign

payload.description: MTA Customer Ambassador Program launched to assist riders ahead of Queens Bus Network Redesign Phase 1

tool_args.raw_text: Buses saw a nearly 20% decrease in LTAs between those periods

tool_args.label: Jamaica Bus Terminal moved from Merrick to 90th Avenue
tool_args.raw_text: The terminal is moving from Merrick Boulevard to a new, state-of-the-art facility on 90th Avenue.

payload.description: Jamaica Bus Terminal moved from Merrick Boulevard to new facility on 90th Avenue

tool_args.label: Jamaica Bus Depot rebuild improves Queens bus service
tool_args.raw_text: The MTA is rebuilding and expanding the Jamaica Bus Depot in Queens to better serve the community and support a modern, growing bus fleet. Once complete, the upgraded depot will handle the operation, maintenance, and storage of up to 300 buses. This will greatly improve bus service throughout Queens.

payload.description: Jamaica Bus Depot rebuild supports modern bus fleet for Queens bus service improvement

tool_args.label: F/M Swap improves subway reliability
tool_args.raw_text: What does this mean for riders? Increased reliability, reduced delays, and shorter travel times.

payload.description: F/M swap increases subway reliability, reduces delays, shortens travel times

tool_args.label: 168th St Interim Terminal serves MTA and NICE bus routes
tool_args.raw_text: A total of 10 MTA bus routes and five Nassau Inter-County Express bus routes now operates from this interim terminal, serving nearly 10,000 daily riders.

payload.description: 168th St Interim Terminal serves 10 MTA bus routes and 5 NICE bus routes, nearly 10,000 daily riders

tool_args.label: R179 and R211 Subway Car Fleets
tool_args.raw_text: our teams have completed the conversion of the A and C lines to all new R179 and R211 cars... We've now begun introducing new R211s on the Crosstown G line and expect the entire G Line fleet to be R211s later this year.

payload.a_line: converted to all R179 and R211 cars
payload.c_line: converted to all R179 and R211 cars
payload.g_line: introducing R211s, entire fleet expected to be R211s later this year

tool_args.label: NaviLens Wayfinding Program
tool_args.raw_text: NaviLens is now available at 44 stations, including several of our large station complexes such as Brooklyn Bridge-City Hall, Canal St, Bleecker St/Broadway-Lafayette, and Union Sq. NaviLens is also available at every Bx12 bus stop and bus shelter in Manhattan and The Bronx. We are thrilled to report over 45,000 uses of NaviLens year to date. NaviLens is now also available on all 6 line subway cars.

tool_args.label: Convo Access ASL Interpretation Pilot
tool_args.raw_text: Convo Access instantly connects people who are Deaf or hard-of-hearing with an American Sign Language interpreter to facilitate conversations with MTA staff. In the three months since the Pilot has launched, we have had over 200 successful calls made

payload.description: ASL interpretation via QR code for Deaf/hard-of-hearing customers

tool_args.label: Paratransit Single-Day Record June 11, 2025
tool_args.raw_text: on June 11, our Paratransit service hit an all-time record, with 46,251 trips scheduled in a single day. This is the first time we've surpassed 46,000 scheduled trips in one day

payload.context: All-time record, first time surpassing 46,000 scheduled trips

tool_args.raw_text: May: Too few police 50%, Right amount 40%, Too many 10%
